hero.title: Commerce
hero.subtitle: A branch of education focused on business, trade, financial systems, and the exchange of goods and services — how money, markets, and economies function in the real world.

streamDistribution.name: Science (PCM/PCB)
streamDistribution.perception: High parental pressure / Perceived high safety

streamDistribution.name: Commerce
streamDistribution.perception: Perceived corporate pathway / Family business continuity

distributionInsights: In rural areas almost everyone wants to be a government officer, which pushes Arts & Humanities to roughly 45% — double the share Commerce gets.
Most students who do pick Commerce admit they didn't really know what else to choose, unsure about Science and doubtful about opportunities in Arts.
Only around 10% of Commerce students say they actually know what they want to do with it.
About 15% avoid other streams purely because they want to escape Math, not because they're drawn to Commerce itself.

whyChooseReasons.translation: Couldn't get or didn't want Science, but felt Arts lacked opportunity
Laser-focused on clearing high-leverage exams like CA for guaranteed stability
Wants a corporate/office career but wants to soften the math load
Needs a baseline understanding of accounts and trade to run a family business
Genuinely driven by startups, digital marketing, and modern finance

myths.title: The "Fallback" Fallacy
myths.myth: Commerce is only for average students who weren't smart enough to get into the Science stream.
myths.reality: This is a purely cultural bias. Science tests your ability to understand physical laws and complex formulas. Commerce tests your ability to understand human behavior, resource allocation, and systemic logic. Choosing Commerce isn't a demotion — it's a pivot toward the language of money and global markets.

myths.title: The "It's an Easy Stream" Trap
myths.myth: There is no Physics or Chemistry, so Commerce must be a breeze.
myths.reality: The shock students face in Class 11 Accountancy is brutal. Balancing complex balance sheets, understanding microeconomic graphs, and navigating corporate law require intense logical discipline — many find it tougher than 12th-grade Science.

myths.title: The "AI Will Replace Accountants" Fear
myths.myth: AI and software are going to do all the accounting and bookkeeping in a few years anyway.
myths.reality: AI will destroy clerical jobs like basic data entry and low-level bookkeeping. But it cannot legally audit a multinational corporation, negotiate a merger, or navigate tax loopholes — the strategic, client-facing advisory roles where the big money is remain strictly human.

myths.title: The "Limited Career Scope" Illusion
myths.myth: Commerce only leads to becoming a CA, a bank clerk, or a useless B.Com degree.
myths.reality: The scope is arguably the widest across all industries — Investment Banking, Actuarial Science, Corporate Law, Management Consulting, Venture Capital, and Quant Trading all sit inside Commerce's elite tracks.

myths.title: The "Math is Optional" Misstep
myths.myth: I hate Math, so I'll take Commerce to escape it.
myths.reality: You can pass high school Commerce without Math, but dropping it locks you out of the highest-paying financial careers on the planet — elite Economics, Quant Finance, and Data Analytics degrees strictly require advanced Mathematics.

traditionalPaths.name: Chartered Accountant (CA) / Company Secretary (CS)
traditionalPaths.desc: Compliance, tax law, local auditing, and book balancing — high friction, massive study time, but safe corporate stability.

traditionalPaths.name: Retail Bank Manager / Corporate Accountant
traditionalPaths.desc: Physical cash operations, local lending, basic ledger entries, regional branch operations.

traditionalPaths.name: Traditional Insurance Actuary
traditionalPaths.desc: Calculating basic life expectancy and vehicle accident risk tables using static historical formulas.

modernPaths.name: Quant Finance Analyst / Algorithmic Trader
modernPaths.desc: Combining advanced statistics, Python coding, and machine learning to build models that execute trades in milliseconds.

modernPaths.name: Venture Capital & Startup Deal Striker
modernPaths.desc: Spotting raw tech talent, analyzing hyper-growth markets, structuring equity deals for early-stage companies.

modernPaths.name: M&A Corporate Lawyer
modernPaths.desc: Operating at the highest legal tier to orchestrate multi-billion dollar tech company takeovers and global asset restructuring.

traditionalVsModern.scoreInsight: Traditional Commerce wins decisively on job security. Everything else — salary growth ceiling, global mobility, remote work flexibility — favors the modern path by a wide margin.
